title: Parallax, Masonry, Inflation, Rubber Band & Pill Input
description: Five live UI demos: scroll depth parallax, CSS native masonry, SVG feMorphology text inflate, elastic rubber-band snap, and spring pill tag input.

drops.title: Depth Scroll Parallax
drops.description: Six layered cards move at different parallax rates as you scroll, creating a convincing 3D depth illusion with no external libraries.
drops.concept: Each card is assigned a depth coefficient; its scroll offset scales proportionally, so deeper cards lag behind while foreground cards race ahead. CSS translateZ reinforces the 3D feel. Parameters — depth scale, spread, and speed — are all live-tunable.
drops.source: Scroll-driven depth parallax pattern (common in Apple product pages and high-end landing pages)

drops.title: CSS Native Masonry Grid
drops.description: grid-template-rows: masonry finally lands in Chrome 132+ — true masonry layout with zero JavaScript.
drops.concept: CSS Grid Level 3 introduces native masonry packing via grid-template-rows: masonry. The browser automatically places items into the densest available slot without any JS column calculation. Falls back gracefully to CSS multi-column in older browsers, detected via CSS.supports().
drops.source: CSS Grid Level 3 spec — native masonry (Chrome 132+, Firefox with flag)

drops.title: SVG Text Inflation
drops.description: SVG feMorphology dilates letter shapes outward on hover — text physically puffs up in four distinct modes.
drops.concept: feMorphology with operator=dilate physically grows the alpha channel of SVG text outward, creating an inflate effect that no CSS can replicate. Combined with feGaussianBlur for softness. Four modes: whole-word inflate, per-character independent, outline stroke swell, and full glow bloom. Radius is animated via rAF springs.
drops.source: SVG feMorphology filter technique (see MDN and Ana Tudor's filter experiments)

drops.title: Elastic Rubber Band Drag
drops.description: Drag any card away and spring physics pulls it back with a visible rubber band — stiffness, damping, and color are live-tunable.
drops.concept: Each card maintains a Hooke's-law spring restoring force toward its origin. On mouse/touch drag, the spring is displaced; on release, velocity carries through damping until the card settles. Canvas draws a bezier rubber band with glow proportional to tension, making the physical constraint visible and satisfying.
drops.source: Spring physics drag pattern popularised by Framer Motion and Apple SwiftUI

interaction.mouse: Click + drag cards, release to snap

drops.title: Spring Pill Tag Input
drops.description: A polished multi-select tag input with spring-scale enter/exit animations, fuzzy search dropdown, and keyboard navigation.
drops.concept: A modern take on the classic email tag input: items spring-scale in (cubic-bezier overshoot) and shrink-fade out on removal. Fuzzy search with highlighted matches, full keyboard navigation (arrow keys, enter, backspace), three layout modes (wrap/scroll/stack), and live JSON output. All in under 200 lines of vanilla JS.
drops.source: Animated pill/chip input pattern — common in Linear, Notion, Vercel, and modern SaaS apps
